title: Stakeholders: PM, CMs removal clause 'unconstitutional'
content: A parliamentary panel noted concerns about the anti-corruption bill's arrest clause. Stakeholders found removing officials after thirty days constitutionally problematic. The draft report suggests a sunset clause for automatic reversal of barring. It also proposes using "suspension" instead of "removal" to avoid stigma. The committee's recommendations will be tabled in Lok Sabha soon.

title: Middle East ceasefire dead as US bombs Iran, Hormuz shuts, missiles fly over Gulf
content: The United States carried out a third round of strikes on Iran after accusing Tehran of attacking a commercial container ship in the Strait of Hormuz. In response, Iran announced that it had once again closed the strategic waterway, stepped up attacks on US military facilities across the Gulf and warned that any retaliation would invite a "severe response."

title: Congress out of Punjab poll race, says BJP leader Fatehjung Singh Bajwa
content: A Punjab BJP leader stated the Congress party is not in the race for 2027. He believes the party will struggle to retain its existing eighteen seats. Congress leaders themselves allege compromises with the current state government. Party officials deny any internal factionalism and express unity for upcoming polls. Unity is crucial for the party, but compromised leaders should be avoided.

title: BCCI discussing Doeschate & Morne Morkel's future as India coaches; T Dilip under fire
content: Indian cricket board may need new support staff after England tour concludes. Assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate and fast bowling coach Morne Morkel are considering leaving. VVS Laxman will lead a separate coaching team for the Zimbabwe series. Fielding coach T Dilip is also under scrutiny due to team performance. The board is discussing options to resolve these potential staff departures.

title: Trump urges Americans to buy Dell, but analysts say Wall Street isn't listening
content: President Trump's Oval Office endorsement of Dell computers coincided with a stock surge. Analysts attribute Dell's stock rise to booming AI server business demand. Michael Dell has now become one of the world's five richest people. Dell Technologies reported strong quarterly results and increased AI revenue expectations. Ethics concerns were raised regarding presidential endorsements of specific companies.

title: When myths become diagnoses: Why mental illnesses remain so misunderstood
content: Despite increasing awareness of mental health, public comprehension still lags behind scientific advancements. Misunderstandings surrounding schizophrenia, dissociative identity disorder, and bipolar disorder lead to widespread stigma. The media often dramatizes these issues, portraying affected individuals as violent or erratic. To combat this, we need to prioritize education and foster empathy, while robust support systems must aid patients and caregivers in their journey toward recovery and respect.

title: Hours after Zuckerberg posted about AI model on Musk’s X, he gets business proposal
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the new Muse Spark 1.1 AI model on X. X's Head of Product Nikita Bier then offered Creator Monetization for his account. This exchange occurred on Elon Musk's platform after Zuckerberg's AI announcement. The Muse Spark 1.1 model is designed for coding and complex AI tasks. Developers can now access this powerful new AI through Meta's API.

title: Burning lemon peels and cloves: The surprising benefits explained
content: For centuries, the practice of burning lemon peels and cloves has provided households with protection from sickness while effectively concealing unpleasant odors. Cloves, rich in eugenol, boast antimicrobial and insect-repellent properties, while the delightful citrus scent from lemon peel oil contains d-limonene, known for its calming effects. However, those with respiratory issues should approach this tradition with caution due to the smoke's potential impact.

title: How floating solar panels gave thousands of Salmon a new home
content: A Chilean salmon farm replaced diesel generators with floating solar panels. This initiative significantly cut emissions and fuel consumption annually. The new system also created a quieter and more sheltered environment for the fish. Thousands of salmon have now gathered beneath the floating solar structures. This project demonstrates renewable energy's unexpected environmental benefits beyond emission reduction.

title: Apple lawsuit reveals what ex-iPhone engineer allegedly took before joining OpenAI
content: Apple has filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing confidential trade secrets. The company alleges a former engineer took sensitive files and knowledge after leaving. OpenAI allegedly encouraged job candidates to share Apple's proprietary information. This information was reportedly used to accelerate OpenAI's own hardware projects. OpenAI denies any interest in acquiring other companies' trade secrets.

title: 3 feared drowned as truck plunges into Ganga in UP's Kannauj
content: A truck broke through bridge railings and plunged into the Ganga river. Three people are feared drowned and one person sustained injuries in the incident. Police and disaster response teams immediately launched a search and rescue operation. Efforts are continuing to locate the missing occupants of the vehicle. The exact cause of the accident is still under investigation.

title: 'I'm devastated': Indian record holder Tejas Nandakumar barred from CWG 2026
content: India's national record holder Tejas Nandakumar has been ruled out of the 2026 Commonwealth Games despite qualifying, after his para sport classification failed to meet eligibility requirements. Speaking exclusively to TimesofIndia.com, the 20-year-old reveals how administrative delays, despite months of international travel and personal expense, shattered his Glasgow dream, while urging systemic reforms to prevent similar heartbreak for future para athletes.

title: Badrinath temple donation theft: U'khand govt orders action against officials
content: Uttarakhand government ordered action against officials for financial irregularities. An inquiry found advance funds released without proper approval from the temple corpus. Separately, a Special Investigation Team is probing alleged donation theft at Badrinath temple. CCTV footage shows a suspended employee suspiciously collecting cash from the donation room. The investigation continues with witness statements and evidence analysis.

title: Search for food takes 4 kids to begging on streets after family dispute
content: A family dispute left four young cousins without parental care and they left home. Two sisters and their cousin were found begging near Safdarjung Hospital in Delhi. The other two sisters were located begging in Maharashtra after boarding a train. Police registered a kidnapping FIR and circulated their details nationwide. The children were safely reunited with their families after the extensive search.

title: Did you know July is named after Julius Caesar?
content: Julius Caesar's legacy extends beyond his famous "Veni, Vidi, Vici" military triumphs. His Gallic Wars conquests significantly expanded Roman territories and wealth for the Republic. Caesar's reforms modernized the Roman state and introduced the Julian Calendar. His assassination on the Ides of March led to further civil war and the Roman Empire. His influence continues to be studied and inspires titles and artistic works.

title: Abhishek Sharma enters record books with bizarre milestone in India's England defeat
content: Abhishek Sharma achieved a unique T20I record during India's fifth match. He played his fifty-third T20I without appearing in Tests or ODIs. England secured a dominant series victory by winning the final match. Jos Buttler's exceptional century powered England's high score. India's bowlers and fielders struggled throughout the disappointing encounter.

title: Jammu and Kashmir: Kashmiri Pandit's eatery sparks 'ghar wapsi' calls
content: Akash Dhar, a visually impaired Kashmiri Pandit, opened a restaurant in Langate. Local residents and his landlord have offered significant support and encouragement. The eatery has attracted customers from various communities and areas. This initiative hopes to inspire other Kashmiri migrants to return home. The restaurant's success highlights a positive community response and integration.

title: Watch: Bison flings man 8 feet into air in dramatic attack at US Yellowstone National Park
content: A man sustained serious injuries after a bull bison charged and flipped him nearly eight feet. The aggressive animal targeted the victim and his grandson while they were walking in Yellowstone. Bystanders shouted and rushed the bison, successfully driving it away from the injured man. Emergency responders transported the man for treatment, reporting significant hip and leg pain.

title: 'Stabbed 10 times': Nursing student who blocked stalker's calls murdered in Chhattisgarh PG
content: A 24-year-old man was arrested for fatally stabbing a 19-year-old pharmacy student. The victim had rejected his advances and blocked his phone number previously. He confronted her in her rented room and attacked her with a knife. The accused had also threatened her brother and her café employer. Police arrested the man after he fled towards Raipur following the incident.

title: Gym belt matches Twisha Sharma's injuries, says AIIMS report
content: An AIIMS Delhi team has submitted an 11-page report to the CBI. Laboratory examinations confirmed skin tissue on the gymnastics belt. This finding matched the injury pattern on Twisha Sharma's neck. The report was submitted in a sealed cover under court orders. This development is significant for the ongoing investigation into the death.

title: 'System engaged in protecting dacoits': Kejriwal on Ram temple donation theft
content: Arvind Kejriwal alleged a cover-up in the Ayodhya temple donation theft case. He accused the system of shielding those responsible for the alleged theft. The Aam Aadmi Party organized a Sundarkand recitation program in Delhi. Kejriwal announced a nationwide signature campaign seeking strict punishment for the accused. The BJP criticized the event as a political gimmick to improve Kejriwal's standing.

title: 'What will be the right time?' Omar Abdullah slams BJP over J&K statehood
content: Omar Abdullah criticized the BJP for repeatedly promising Jammu and Kashmir statehood. He alleged the party exploits divisions to gain power in various Indian states. Abdullah stated restoring statehood is a commitment, not a favor to the people. He challenged any BJP candidate to deny promising statehood restoration during elections. The National Conference leader highlighted the BJP's selective memory regarding election promises.

title: Messi involved in heated exchange with referee during Argentina's World Cup win
content: Lionel Messi had a tense moment with the referee during Argentina's quarterfinal match. Argentina secured a dramatic extra-time victory against Switzerland to advance. Alexis Mac Allister opened the scoring early for Argentina with a header. Switzerland equalized after the break, but Breel Embolo received a red card. Julián Álvarez and Lautaro Martínez scored in extra time to seal the win.

title: Bomb threat at Mumbai's Taj Mahal Palace Hotel triggers security scare
content: Mumbai Police are investigating a bomb threat received by the Taj Mahal Palace Hotel on Sunday. Navi Mumbai Police received the call around midnight and immediately alerted Mumbai authorities. A thorough search of the hotel premises was conducted following the received threat. This incident occurred one day after a similar hoax bomb threat at Delhi's Red Fort. Authorities are actively working to trace the origin of both recent threat calls.

title: UK homebuyers on sidelines: Why British house prices could remain stagnant for longer
content: House prices in the United Kingdom are projected to face a prolonged period of stagnation. With high mortgage rates and ongoing geopolitical conflicts dampening buyer enthusiasm, the estate market is bracing for subdued activity during the summer season. Additionally, domestic political uncertainty adds to the financial markets' apprehension. A recovery in this sector will largely depend on improved stability in interest rates and the global economic landscape.

title: Peeing in open: Tinsukia displays violators on LED
content: Tinsukia Municipal Board now displays public urination footage on an LED screen. This initiative aims to deter repeat offenders and promote civic hygiene. Fines previously collected did not effectively change public behavior. Civil defence volunteers assist in recording and displaying these violations. The campaign seeks to encourage residents towards better sanitation practices.

title: 'If that's a free kick...': Haaland fumes over VAR call in World Cup exit
content: Erling Haaland's World Cup debut ended with Norway's quarterfinal loss to England. He scored seven goals, establishing himself as a top tournament scorer. Haaland described the experience as unforgettable and the coolest six weeks of his life. He believes Norway's performance has put the nation on the football map. The striker is now looking forward to a well-deserved break.

title: Jude makes England's World cup song better as Messi looms...
content: Day 31 saw Jude Bellingham drag England past Norway, Argentina stumble through Switzerland and Lionel Messi demand respect from the referee.

title: China leaps ahead with reusable rocket booster, India’s NGLV years away
content: China demonstrated orbital rocket booster recovery, joining reusable launch system developers. India's Next Generation Launch Vehicle program is still years from a demonstration flight. This achievement narrows the gap with global leaders like SpaceX and Blue Origin. Recovering and reusing rocket stages significantly reduces launch costs and increases frequency. India is committed to reusable launchers through its NGLV program.

title: Watch: Ukrainian woman shields child as Russian strikes hit Sumy
content: Russian airstrikes struck Ukraine's northern city of Sumy, killing five and injuring thirty people. Glide bombs also injured ten in the southeastern city of Zaporizhzhia. Kyiv witnessed an overnight attack which wounded twelve civilians and damaged infrastructure. Ukraine has increased drone attacks on Russian supply lines in occupied southern Ukraine. These drone strikes aim to pressure Russia into peace talks on equal footing.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 3 [LIVE]: Comedy franchise locks Rs 50 crore India net in 3 days
content: 'Dhamaal 4', starring Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh and Jaaved Jaaferi, has opened to a strong response at the box office, riding on the nostalgia of the nearly two-decade-old comedy franchise. After a Day 2 net of Rs 22.50 crore, marking a 60.7% jump from opening day, the film has entered Day 3 with live tracking showing it inching towards the Rs 50 crore India gross mark within its opening weekend itself.

title: Daughter-in-law accuses former MP Vinayak Raut, family of harassment
content: Vinayak Raut and six others face charges following a complaint by his daughter-in-law. Girija Raut alleged physical and mental harassment after her marriage in December 2017. She claimed occult rituals and unidentified pills were administered to help her conceive. Girija stated prolonged mental harassment prevented her from speaking out earlier. Police have arrested one accused, Firoz Baba, and are investigating further.

title: Trisha Krishnan shares heartbreaking note and rare BTS moments from '96'
content: Actress Trisha Krishnan shared a heartfelt note remembering legendary singer S. Janaki. She also released rare behind-the-scenes photos from the film '96. S. Janaki appeared in a deleted scene which fans later saw and discussed. This scene was removed from the final cut due to runtime constraints. Fans hope her memory will be honored in the upcoming '96 sequel.

title: 'It will be harsh to say ... ': Ex-India batter on Shreyas Iyer's future as captain
content: Shreyas Iyer's T20I captaincy faces scrutiny after recent series defeats. Former player Abhishek Nayar urges patience, emphasizing captaincy development time. He believes Iyer needs freedom to build his own team effectively. Nayar suggests judging Iyer's leadership after he shapes the squad. Patience is advised as India enters a new T20 cricket phase.

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Cloudburst floods hotels, houses in Pahalgam; heavy to very heavy rain lash parts of India
content: Continuous heavy rainfall on July 11 triggered a major landslide scare in Shimla’s Sanjauli area, forcing residents of Bothwell Estate to remain outdoors since around 3 a.m amid fears that their houses could collapse

title: S. Janaki’s demise: Maestro Ilaiyaraaja, singer P. Susheela pay rich tributes
content: Recalling their long musical association, Ilaiyaraaja described Janaki as an exceptionally hardworking artiste.

title: PM Modi’s Statehood promise should carry weight: Omar Abdullah
content: National Conference has announced a sit-in at New Delhi’s Jantar Mantar on July 20, the first day of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, to demand restoration of Statehood and constitutional safeguards

title: S. Janaki who had the voice for every human emotion
content: She possessed the rare ability to convey every shade of human emotion. Her songs remain woven into the cultural memory of south India.

title: S. Janaki: The Isai Arasi who established the voice of the rural, female heroine
content: Janaki amma’s voice gave contour to the heroines, their fears, their desires, their hopes and their dreams, even as Bharathiraja wrote them lion’s space on the screen

title: Goyal to visit Spain, Belgium, Finland this week
content: Delegation comprises leading Indian companies across sectors, including advanced manufacturing, clean energy, digital technologies, gems and jewellery, food processing, healthcare, and design, says Commerce Ministry

title: Vietnam boat tragedy: Injured A.P. tourist recovering, repatriation of mortal remains of the deceased underway
content: Embassy of India in Vietnam has finalised the authorised agency that will facilitate the repatriation of the mortal remains of the deceased to India

title: Piyush Goyal | Crisis manager
content: The Commerce Minister finds himself on the frontline when the government is busy defending the U.S. trade deal which critics say is one-sided

title: New voters have to also submit parents' SIR details for inclusion in electoral rolls
content: The functionary underlined that the declaration was added through instructions and Form 6 has not been amended

title: Need to train more skilled cardiovascular technologists to meet rising cardiac care demand: C.N. Manjunath
content: Bengaluru Rural MP C.N. Manjunath said advanced cardiac centres should support peripheral hospitals so that patients in rural and remote areas can access timely treatment

title: Constable cycling to ‘Sunday with Cycle’ event killed in Ongole car crash
content: Armed Reserve Constable Surabhi Mariyadasu was cycling to the District Police Office when a speeding car hit him from behind; the car was allegedly driven by a postgraduate medical student

title: S. Janaki (1938-2026) — A collection recalling the singer's pan-India career
content: S. Janaki won the National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for “Senthoora Poove” from 16 Vayathinile, the debut directorial venture of Bharathiraja, who passed away last month

title: Telangana to conduct Statewide deworming drive for 96.81 lakh children on July 13
content: The uniform administration of Albendazole tablets on a single day will help eliminate soil-transmitted helminth infections among children, who are considered the vulnerable age group, the health department said

title: Congress seeks to recast Ayodhya narrative amid Ram Temple donation row
content: As the Ram Temple donation controversy unfolds, the Congress targets the Trust’s management and seeks to shift the political narrative around Ayodhya by demanding accountability and transparency

title: Cloudburst triggers flooding in Pahalgam stream, PDP flags ‘wanton illegal construction’ issue
content: A sudden spell of intense rainfall, possibly caused by a localised cloudburst, struck the forest areas of Awoora and Dehwathu in Pahalgam, in south Kashmir's Anantnag district, says officials

title: When Janaki became the constant soundtrack of a family in Kerala
content: Abhilash Pudukad can hardly recall a childhood memory without the small vintage Sanyo tape recorder and S. Janaki’s mellifluous voice playing in the background

title: Electrification of Pattukottai-Karaikudi broad gauge section nearing completion
content: With the completiton of the work on the 70-km stretch, all lines  in Tiruchi Railway Division will be open to running electric locomotives; notification issued asking public in the area to be cautious

title: Janaki second to none in talent, says Ilayaraaja ahead of singer's last rites in Mysuru | LIVE
content: The mortal remains are placed at Mysuru's Maharaja’s College Ground for the public to pay their last respects; last rites to be held this evening (July 12, 2026)

title: CPI hardens its stance on Deputy Leader of the Opposition post in Kerala Assembly
content: Party’s State secretary Binoy Viswam says it is necessary for Left Democratic Front’s unity; he adds that the demand is intended to strengthen the front

title: TMC's Abhishek Banerjee booked after woman loses leg, blames treatment at Sebaashray health camp
content: The Diamond Harbour MP unveiled the initiative on January 2 when the Trinamool Congress was in power in the State. Already, two FIRs have been filed against him in connection with alleged irregularities in Sebaashray health camps

title: Shabad killing spree: Police SI suspended; seven special teams formed to nab perpetrator
content: A man accused in a POCSO case allegedly went on a killing spree in Shabad, stabbing six people to death, including his wife, two young sons, and three members of the complainant’s family, before fleeing

title: I am neither running away nor hiding: DMK leader E.V. Velu
content: Displaying his medical records, Mr. Velu said he had been receiving treatment for heart ailments at Mount Elizabeth Hospital in Singapore since 2016. After election campaign, he had planned to undergo medical check-up there, stated the DMK whip in the Assembly.

title: Ram Temple trust to decide CEO’s powers, no govt. interference: Nripendra Misra
content: He also claimed that there have been no complaints from devotees visiting the Ram temple in Ayodhya, and that they feel positively about their experience

title: Ram Temple donation row: RSS expresses concern over ‘misuse of funds’, voices confidence in SIT probe
content: Delegates, at the regional convenors’ meeting in Belagavi, underscored the need for measures that ensure that such incidents do not recur

title: S. Janaki’s death an irreparable loss to music, says Andhra Pradesh CM
content: The playback singer, who hailed from Repalle in Bapatla district and won four National Awards, will be cremated in Mysuru with full official honours; leaders across parties paid tributes

title: Congress recalls former NZ PM’s role in reviving ties with India in the 1980s as Modi elevates partnership
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh also recalled New Zealand’s role in helping develop the Indian dairy sector, establish AIIMS; says former NZ PM David Lange built close ties with Indira and Rajiv Gandhi

title: PM Modi returned with diplomatic gains, Rahul Gandhi 'conspiring' abroad: BJP
content: "Just think what New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon said. And the Congress party in India refuses to accept it. I think our Opposition should also learn something," says Sambit Patra

title: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership; target ₹35,000 crore trade by 2030
content: A joint statement noted that Narendra Modi and Christopher Luxon exchanged views on the Indo-Pacific, reaffirming their shared commitment to a free, open, and prosperous region

title: Karnataka Transport Minister goes incognito on BMTC buses, orders action against staff
content: During the inspection, Mr. Suresh found a conductor and a driver allegedly derelict in their duties and recommended their suspension

title: Tiruchi residents among those killed in Vietnam boat accident
content: N. Balaji, A.M. Sheikh Abdullah, and S. Alagurajan, were residents of Tiruchi who were killed in the Vietnam boat accident

title: Two workers die in house collapse
content: Parts of the house, including the roof and a wall, suddenly collapsed, trapping two workers under the soil

title: Poor monsoon and no Cauvery water: distress in Tamil Nadu’s delta region
content: All is not well in the famed ‘Granary of South India’. Between a poor southwest  monsoon and lack of water in the Cauvery, crops and farmers are left high and dry. The kuruvai cultivation area in Thanjavur alone has dipped to around 89,000 acres this season from around 1,94,000 acres in the previous cultivation year.  This report describes the situation on the ground

title: Push cart vendor found murdered in park in Bengaluru
content: The deceased has been identified as Rakesh alias Rocky (20), son of late Kumar and a resident of Rajyotsava Nagar in Kumaraswamy Layout

title: Amit Shah vows 20% increase in Gandhinagar's green cover by 2029
content: Home Minister said that 'Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam', the campaign to increase India's green cover, has turned into a mass movement

title: What a dolphin’s death in Kerala reveals about India’s marine plastic pollution
content: Necropsy of an endangered Indian Ocean humpback dolphin that washed ashore on Kozhikode beach recently reveals fatal plastic fishing net ingestion, highlighting growing threats from marine pollution and ‘ghost nets’. CMFRI data show marine mammal strandings remain high, with coastal dolphins among the species most vulnerable

title: Tackling marine debris and microplastics pollution: tough challenges ahead
content: Andrew Booth, Chief Scientist and Research Manager, Observation and Ecosystem group at SINTEF Ocean, Norway was in Thiruvananthapuram last week to direct a workshop on ‘Marine Debris And Microplastics Pollution: Assessment And Characterisation’

title: Murray & Co and its Sunday auction: the famous ‘hammer’ of Madras nears the century mark
content: Since February 10, 1927, the hammer at the auction house Murray & Co has not had a break except for two seasons: for a few weeks during a relocation to a new address and then during the pandemic. The operational space might have changed a few times, but its place in people’s mind space has not

title: Husband arrested for woman's death at Mussoorie homestay
content: The husband, an IT firm employee, was apprehended from the Madhu Vihar area of east Delhi after a non-bailable warrant was obtained from the court of the Judicial Magistrate, Mussoorie

title: Football fans in Kerala transform their homes into colourful tributes to their teams
content: For these two fans, who live near Kochi, who have painted their houses in the colours of the countries they support in the FiFA World Cup, football is more than a seasonal obsession

title: National Medical Commission approves 100 additional MBBS seats for Tamil Nadu
content: It has permitted the government medical colleges in Namakkal and Tiruppur to increase their annual MBBS intake by 50 seats each from the academic year 2026-27

title: CJP protest day 23: Wangchuk’s health worsens as fast enters 15th day
content: The CJP stated that several political leaders and public intellectuals were scheduled to visit the protest site during the day to express solidarity with the agitation

title: Landslides will continue unless there is accountability, say scientists, environmentalists
content: They point out that the recent episodes are a result of a combination of geological factors, meteorological and topographical changes

title: VCK turns its focus to Tamil nationalism post-election
content: VCK founder and Chidambaram MP Thol. Thirumavalavan has urged the party to gear up for a ‘Tamil nationalist’ public rally on August 17 in Ulundurpet near Villupuram

title: ‘Punjab’s painful past is not a script to be selectively edited to suit a narrative,’ says Minister Bittu
content: “Makers of Satluj cannot hide behind the excuse of “creative freedom” while presenting disputed claims as established history,” says Ravneet Singh Bittu

title: Karnataka police clarify SC/ST grievance meetings are for the public, not police personnel
content: The police said the earlier reference to “police officers and personnel” was incorrect and has now been rectified. The meetings are exclusively intended as a public outreach initiative to safeguard the rights and welfare of SC/ST citizens.

title: How a single ₹500 counterfeit currency helped police unravel a larger syndicate
content: The kingpin and the accused arrested in Bengaluru allegedly printed the counterfeit currency and roped in foot soldiers to circulate the notes

content: The MEA went on to describe as “deeply worrisome”, the “continuing incidents of attacks on commercial shipping in the region”

title: Supreme Court to hear plea seeking guidelines to curb 'unpredictable fluctuations' of airfares on July 13
content: A bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta will hear the plea filed by social activist S. Laxminarayanan, who also sought a robust and independent regulator that ensures transparency and passenger protection across the civil aviation sector

title: Youth Congress worker assault case: Kerala Government seeks legal opinion to prosecute ADGP M.R. Ajith Kumar
content: ‘Home Minister Ramesh Chennithala has referred the legally and politically fraught question to the State Advocate General (AG)’s office based on a statement of facts filed by the charging officer in the case, Baiju Paulose, in the Alappuzha district court in June,’ says Officials

title: Police, bulldozers deployed against Amaravati land-givers, alleges Jagan Mohan Reddy
content: The YSRCP chief demands that the government should refrain from acquiring even a cent of land without the farmers’ consent

title: Tamil Nadu government seeks assistance from Union government for MRTS revamp in Chennai
content: ₹6,600-crore project is aimed at transforming  MRTS to Chennai Metro standards by refurbishing stations and upgrading trains; T.N. urges Union govt. to consider plan as ‘Central sector’ project

content: Students and instructors of Fatah Combat System (FCS), a martial arts training institution, have been arrested in the anti-terror probe in Bangladesh

content: Kerala has ample reason to celebrate. Palakkad district can now look with pride at a remarkable trio.

content: The former soldier said that after serving in three wars, he never imagined he would have to make rounds of government offices to save his land.

content: The woman alleged that the delivery agent had visited her house to deliver a package and then asked her if he could use the toilet.

content: The destroyed drugs included 39.068 kg of morphine, 79.770 kg of opium, 58.877 kg of heroin, 0.0471 kg of cocaine, and 37,796.34 kg of cannabis (ganja).

content: With recently authorised "pet friendly" weddings, Ecuadoran officials are embracing the role of furry friends in modern families.

content: The court cleared the 40-year-old accused of all charges under the Indian Penal Code and POCSO Act, citing inconsistencies and lack of corroborative evidence.

content: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Updates: The speedboat was ferrying 32 Indian tourists, three crew members and one Vietnamese tour guide from May Rut Ngoai Islet to An Thoi International Port

content: According to the Delhi Police, the accused shooters were receiving information through a network based abroad, following which they were allegedly planning to execute the crime.

title: One Tunnel, One Highway: Rs 14,115-Crore Bet To Transform UP, Delhi-NCR
content: While Delhi gets a six-lane tunnel, for Uttar Pradesh, the Cabinet has approved the 117.7-km Kanpur-Kabrai greenfield highway worth Rs 7,145.14 crore.

content: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

title: Former Qatar Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani Dies At 74
content: Sheikh Hamad, who stepped down in June 2013 after 18 years as emir, was the architect of energy-rich Qatar's stunning ambitions that turned it from a backwater into an international crossroads in less than a generation.

title: Earthquake Of Magnitude 4.5 Hits Bay Of Bengal, Tremors Felt In Andhra
content: The tremors were felt in Gajuwaka, Mangalapalem, Sagar Nagar, Pedda Waltair, Arilova, MVP colony, Appughar and other areas.

title: 'Dawood Planted Bomb At Taj': Hoax Call Triggers Security Scare In Mumbai
content: According to officials, the Taj Hotel was placed on high alert after, at around 12:13 am, a man called the Navi Mumbai Police Control Room and allegedly claimed, "Dawood has planted a bomb at the Taj Hotel".

title: Indian Missing, 10 Rescued After Iran Attacks Ship In Strait Of Hormuz
content: India described the continued attacks on ships as deeply worrisome and called for an immediate de-escalation in the Middle East.

title: Why The Amarnath Ice Lingam Is Melting Faster Than Ever Before
content: According to several reports, the ice lingam has melted by over 90% and has almost disappeared just days into the 57-day Amarnath yatra.

title: Iran Shuts Hormuz After Attack On 'Unauthorised' Ship, US Begins New Strikes
content: Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was closed "until further notice" and until "the end of US interference in this region."
